rationale: The cited paper appears only as a single literature-review mention in a grouped sentence in the General Discussion, where the authors list prior advances on really new products. It is used to note that prior work proposed a measure for really new product preference, but the citing paper does not build hypotheses, measures, analyses, or interpretation directly from that paper. Because the citation functions as background positioning rather than an active conceptual or methodological input, this is best classified as mention-only.
